data.title: Anheuser-Busch launches a new vodka tea brand called Skimmers
data.description:
Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) launched its new vodka tea RTD product this week, according to industry reports. The Skimmers brand will be sold in various 4 and 8 packs. Flavors will include original, half & half, peach, and lemonade tea. The vodka will be sourced from the company's Cutwater brand.

 

 

The vodka tea product is 4.5% ABV, with 100 calories and 2 grams of sugar. Skimmers will initially be available in 18 states and D.C., with a higher concentration in the Midwest, Rockies, and Florida, according to Evercore ISI analyst Robert Ottenstein. Notably, Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) has plans for further expansion for the brand. 

Ottenstein and his team believe that the Skimmers product will help Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) compete better with Surfside and Boston Beer's (SAM) Sun Cruiser, which were noted to be riding strong hard tea category trends. Sazerac's Firefly Sweet Tea Vodka, Heaven Hill Brands' Deep Eddy Sweet Tea Vodka, High Noon, and Boston Beer's (SAM) Twisted Tea are other competitors in the category.

data.title: Greenwich Life rises on data from late-stage trial for breast cancer vaccine
data.description:
Greenwich LifeSciences (NASDAQ:GLSI) added ~23% in the premarket on Wednesday after the company posted preliminary immune response data from a Phase 3 clinical trial for GLSI-100, a vaccine designed to prevent the recurrence of breast cancer.

The FLAMINGO-01 trial enrolled patients with HER2-positive breast cancer who continued to have the residual disease or those who were at risk of developing the disease despite a pathologic complete response at the surgery.

Citing early data from FLAMINGO-01, the Stafford, Texas-based biotech said there were immune responses in both HLA-A*02 patients and non-HLA-A*02 patients who were part of GLSI-100/placebo arms and a third open-label arm, respectively.

"We are very encouraged to see that the preliminary results from FLAMINGO-01 show immune responses in both HLA-A*02 and non-HLA-A*02 patients,” CEO Snehal Patel.

The company said it is considering the advantages of adding a randomized placebo arm for the non-HLA-A*02 group to convert that open-label arm into a potentially second pivotal and blinded Phase III trial.

“If successful, the company could pursue approval for both HLA-A*02 and non-HLA-A*02 patients in similar time frames,” said Patel, adding that it could potentially double the market for GP-2, a key component in GLSI-100, up to $10B in annual revenue.

data.title: Stock index futures in red as Trump's upcoming tariffs take center stage
data.description:
Stock index futures were in the red on Wednesday, as investors braced for President Trump's reciprocal tariff plans.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.7%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.7%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.5%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.18%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.89%.

Wall Street's major averages finished mixed Tuesday in the jumpstart of Q2 action, as investors waited for the Trump administration's “Liberation Day” plan to impose large-scale tariffs on trading partners worldwide.

U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday is slated to formally announce "country-based" tariffs during an appearance in the Rose Garden at 4 pm ET.

"We clearly don’t know any of the details, including which countries will be targeted and at what rate, with reporting yesterday suggesting that a final decision was still to be made," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The U.S. Trade Representative's office is preparing a third tier of tariffs that would be lower than a potential 20% universal levy option, The Wall Street Journal reported Tuesday.

Ahead of today’s announcement, fears about stagflation in financial markets continued to mount, even if markets had a pretty positive day yesterday. The stagflation fears were exacerbated by the latest batch of U.S. data, Reid added. 

"The erratic nature of trade policy, uncertainty about whether legal procedures will be followed, limited clarity about end-objectives, the possibility that Trump will again retreat from some taxes, and the unpredictability of patronage-style deals to gain exemptions create uncertainty about the direct effect of the taxes," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The second-round effects depend on unpredictable reactions by U.S. companies, a far more complex global trade system than existed 20 years ago, and U.S. consumers whose reaction to inflation has shifted, Donovan added. 

The economic calendar is on the lighter side today, with mainly the March ADP Jobs Report and the February Factory Orders slated to land. The former is expected to come in at 118K, and the latter at 0.5% M/M.

data.title: Kalo Gold looks to raise C$2M in private placement
data.description:
• Kalo Gold (TSXV:KALO:CA) said it intends to conduct a private placement to raise up to C$2M. 
  • The firm said it will offer up to 40M units at C$0.05 apiece, and each unit will have one share and one share purchase warrant.
  • Each warrant will entitle the holder thereof to acquire an additional share at an exercise price of $0.08 apiece for a period of twenty-four months from the date of issuance.

data.title: Top U.S. consulting firms are said to offer cost cuts amid federal scrutiny
data.description:
The largest consulting firms working with the U.S. federal government have pledged to reduce billions of dollars from their contracts in an effort to support cost-cutting initiatives — but the Trump administration is pressing for even greater savings, The Wall Street Journal reported on Wednesday.

Major firms such as Accenture (NYSE:ACN), Booz Allen Hamilton (NYSE:BAH), Deloitte, IBM (NYSE:IBM) and others met a General Services Administration deadline this week to justify their existing federal contracts and highlight areas where costs could be reduced. However, sources familiar with the process say officials from the Trump administration are expected to request deeper reductions and possibly renegotiate some terms, the Journal reported.

While some firms provided extensive documentation without identifying enough concrete savings, others outlined substantial cost-cutting opportunities. One firm reportedly proposed as much as $12 billion in potential savings for the government.

Booz Allen Hamilton (NYSE:BAH), which earns nearly all of its $11 billion in yearly revenue from federal work, suggested over $1 billion in potential savings. Chief Executive Horacio Rozanski said the company flagged contracts that could be reviewed or canceled and offered multiple recommendations to improve government operations. Among their suggestions were transitioning more contracts to performance-based pricing and considering government in-sourcing for certain tasks to boost efficiency.

Leidos (NYSE:LDOS), another prominent federal contractor, also submitted a set of detailed proposals aimed at improving government efficiency, according to a company spokesperson, though specific savings figures were not disclosed.

Accenture and IBM declined to comment, while Deloitte didn't respond to inquiries by the Journal.

The administration’s review comes as part of a broader effort to curb federal spending, launched earlier this year. Officials requested that the top 10 federal consulting providers justify their roles and expenses. These firms are expected to collect more than $65 billion in fees through 2025 and beyond.

The initiative has hurt investor confidence, with shares of Booz Allen (BAH) and Accenture (NYSE:ACN) falling around 40% and 12% respectively since Trump’s election. Leidos (NYSE:LDOS) shares are down about 30% in the same period. In contrast, the broader Standard & Poor's 500 index (SP500) has declined about 5%.

Federal agencies overseeing the contracts will now assess the firms’ proposals, and the GSA has indicated it will compare each firm's submission to those of its competitors.

Many contractors are already experiencing the effects of the administration’s spending clampdown. Accenture (ACN) Chief Executive Julie Sweet said last week that the tightening of procurement processes has slowed down new federal business, leading to negative impacts on revenue and sales, the Journal reported.

data.title: Schwab upgraded to Buy at Citi on prospects for renewed NNA growth story
data.description:
Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) stock gained 1.5% in Wednesday premarket trading after Citi upgraded the online broker's stock to Buy from Neutral as the firm pivots back to offense after completing its integration of Ameritrade.

 With the company now investing for growth, management is increasingly confident that net new asset ("NNA") growth can continue to rise in 2025 and head toward its 5%-7% long-term growth rate. Schwab's (NYSE:SCHW) NNA  growth, on an annualized basis, was 5.6%, Citi points out.

"We see potential to return to an NNA/organic growth story, which, if executed while maintaining rate sensitivity/balance sheet discipline, should translate to multiple appreciation over time," Citi analyst Christopher Allen wrote in a note to clients. "Also, given SCHW’s capital generation capacity, we see an improving capital return story as well."

Schwab (SCHW) has also been making progress on paying down short-term funding. When combined with the improving NNA, balance sheet flexibility should increase, Allen added.

"Looking longer-term, we see a healthy capital return story playing out, with SCHW likely to follow the recent TD share repurchases in future quarters/years with increasing buybacks," the analyst said.

Citi raised its price target for SCHW to $102 from $85, representing 17x Citi's 2027 EPS estimate.

The Buy rating contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Hold and aligns with the average SA Analyst rating and the average Wall Street rating, both at Buy.

data.title: European markets retreat as Trump's tariff decision looms
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.10am ET.

London (UKX) -0.79% to 8,567. 

Germany (DAX:IND) -1.28% to 22,251. 

France (CAC:IND) -0.61% to 7,828. France’s government budget deficit narrowed to €40.3B in February.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 0.81% lower to 535.28, as investors await President Donald Trump’s latest round of tariffs, with reports suggesting that Trump may slap a universal 20% tariff on most imports, though a country-specific reciprocal approach remains the likely outcome.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on the economic calendar.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down less than 1 basis point to 4.15%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 2 basis points to 2.66%.

UK's 10-year yield was up less than 1 basis point to 4.63%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).

data.title: RPC expands Permian operations with $245M acquisition of Pintail Completions
data.description:
RPC Inc. (NYSE:RES) said Wednesday it agreed to acquire Permian Basin provider of oilfield wireline services Pintail Completions for $245M in cash and stock.

Pintail operates more than 30 active fleets, and its conventional and electric wireline units are among the newest in the industry, and its FY 2024 revenues totaled $409M, according to RPC (NYSE:RES).

RPC (RES) said it expects the acquisition will be accretive 2025 earnings per share and operating and free cash flow.

"Pintail Completions is recognized as an industry leader in wireline perforation services, complementing our existing completion service lines and making wireline a meaningful component of RPC's portfolio," President and CEO Ben Palmer said.

data.title: Allegion acquires Trimco Hardware
data.description:
• Allegion (NYSE:ALLE) said it has acquired privately held Trimco Hardware, including its brands and other assets.
  • The terms of the deal were not disclosed. 
  • Trimco will report into the Allegion Americas segment, which is led by Allegion senior vice president Dave Ilardi.
  • The firm added Jason Bennett, owner of Trimco, will join Allegion as general manager of Trimco.

data.title: BlackBerry beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 and FY26 outlook
data.description:
• BlackBerry press release (NYSE:BB): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.03 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $141.7M (-18.1% Y/Y) beats by $8.89M.

  • Operating cash flow beat expectations and improved by $57 million year-over-year to $42.0 million.

    • Total company adjusted EBITDA exceeded previously-provided guidance at $21.1 million.

  • Total cash and investments increased by $144 million sequentially to $410 million, in part due to the improved operating cash flow, and in part due to approximately $80 million received from first tranche of cash from the sale of Cylance to Arctic Wolf.
